# Taxonomy Checklist: WSDL Test Assets

**Purpose**: Validate whether the fixture taxonomy and classification requirements are complete, clear, consistent, and ready for planning and implementation
**Created**: 2026-04-06
**Feature**: [spec.md](../spec.md)

**Note**: This checklist is generated by the `/speckit.checklist` command based on feature context and requirements.

## Requirement Completeness

- [x] CHK001 Are all six category names explicitly fixed in the requirements with no missing or extra taxonomy buckets? [Completeness, Spec §FR-001]
- [x] CHK002 Does the spec define where the taxonomy lives in the repository and whether that root path is mandatory rather than illustrative? [Completeness, Spec §FR-002, Contract §Root Path]
- [x] CHK003 Are the valid categories `simplest`, `simple`, `complex`, and `very-complex` each described by documented structural traits rather than left partially implicit? [Completeness, Spec §FR-003a, Research §Valid Complexity Taxonomy Strategy]
- [x] CHK004 Does the requirements set define what makes `invalid-wsdls` distinct from valid categories beyond merely saying it contains failures? [Completeness, Spec §FR-005a, Contract §invalid-wsdls]
- [x] CHK005 Are requirements present for the import-based category to state both when XSD companions are required and how they relate to the primary WSDL fixture? [Completeness, Spec §FR-006, Contract §Fixture-Set Layout]

## Requirement Clarity

- [x] CHK006 Are the boundaries between `simplest` and `simple` quantified clearly enough that a planner could classify borderline one-file WSDLs consistently? [Clarity, Research §Valid Complexity Taxonomy Strategy, Ambiguity]
- [x] CHK007 Are the distinctions between `complex` and `very-complex` stated with concrete structural traits instead of subjective intensity words alone? [Clarity, Spec §FR-009, Research §Valid Complexity Taxonomy Strategy]
- [x] CHK008 Is the phrase "structurally incorrect WSDL" defined clearly enough to exclude malformed non-WSDL XML and valid WSDLs with only inconvenient test semantics? [Clarity, Spec §FR-005a, Contract §invalid-wsdls]
- [x] CHK009 Are the naming rules specific enough to determine whether abbreviations, numerals, and repeated category hints are allowed in fixture-set names? [Clarity, Spec §FR-004, Contract §Naming Rules, Gap]
- [x] CHK010 Does the spec make it clear whether category semantics are normative requirements or informative examples for contributors? [Clarity, Spec §FR-003a, Contract §Category Semantics, Ambiguity]

## Requirement Consistency

- [x] CHK011 Are category names and spellings consistent across the spec, plan, research, quickstart, and directory contract? [Consistency, Spec §FR-001, Plan §Project Structure, Contract §Category Directories]
- [x] CHK012 Do the category definitions in research align with the contract's category semantics without introducing conflicting placement rules? [Consistency, Research §Valid Complexity Taxonomy Strategy, Contract §Category Semantics]
- [x] CHK013 Are invalid-fixture rules consistent between the user stories, edge cases, functional requirements, and assumptions? [Consistency, Spec §User Story 3, Spec §FR-005a, Spec §Assumptions]
- [x] CHK014 Do the import-based taxonomy rules consistently treat XSD companions as part of the same fixture set rather than as shared assets across categories? [Consistency, Spec §FR-006, Contract §Stability Expectations]

## Acceptance Criteria Quality

- [x] CHK015 Can taxonomy success be objectively evaluated from the current success criteria without relying on undocumented reviewer judgment? [Acceptance Criteria, Spec §SC-001, Spec §SC-002]
- [x] CHK016 Is "classify correctly" measurable enough to determine what evidence would show a reviewer or implementer could place a fixture in the right category? [Measurability, Spec §SC-002, Ambiguity]
- [x] CHK017 Do the acceptance scenarios verify that category definitions are usable for classification, not just that folders exist? [Acceptance Criteria, Spec §User Story 1]

## Scenario Coverage

- [x] CHK018 Are requirements defined for both happy-path taxonomy use cases and negative-path classification cases such as invalid WSDL placement? [Coverage, Spec §User Story 1, Spec §User Story 3]
- [x] CHK019 Does the taxonomy explicitly cover how self-contained valid fixtures differ from valid fixtures that depend on imported XSD files? [Coverage, Spec §FR-006, Contract §very-complex-wsdls-with-xsd-imports]
- [x] CHK020 Are requirements present for how contributors should handle a fixture whose characteristics appear to span multiple valid categories? [Coverage, Spec §Edge Cases, Spec §FR-009]

## Edge Case Coverage

- [x] CHK021 Is the tie-break rule for fixtures that could fit multiple valid categories specific enough to avoid inconsistent placement across contributors? [Edge Case, Spec §Edge Cases, Ambiguity]
- [x] CHK022 Does the spec define whether structurally invalid WSDLs may also include otherwise complex features, or whether invalidity overrides all valid taxonomy placement? [Edge Case, Spec §Edge Cases, Spec §FR-005, Gap]
- [x] CHK023 Are requirements stated for how to classify fixtures that are structurally simple but include imported XSD files, or is that boundary intentionally excluded? [Edge Case, Research §Valid Complexity Taxonomy Strategy, Gap]

## Dependencies & Assumptions

- [x] CHK024 Are the assumptions about cross-platform path safety and ASCII naming tied back to taxonomy requirements that depend on readable category and fixture-set names? [Assumption, Spec §FR-004, Spec §Assumptions]
- [x] CHK025 Does the spec document whether future tests are expected to depend on taxonomy stability, making category renames a controlled change rather than an incidental cleanup? [Dependency, Contract §Stability Expectations, Gap]

## Ambiguities & Conflicts

- [x] CHK026 Is there any unresolved conflict between the directory contract's detailed category semantics and the spec's higher-level structural-trait language? [Conflict, Spec §FR-003a, Contract §Category Semantics]
- [x] CHK027 Are the words `simple`, `complex`, and `very-complex` supported by enough non-subjective criteria to avoid interpretation drift over time? [Ambiguity, Spec §FR-009, Research §Valid Complexity Taxonomy Strategy]
- [x] CHK028 Does the specification clearly state whether taxonomy definitions may evolve later or are intended to be frozen for this feature's initial delivery? [Ambiguity, Spec §FR-001, Contract §Stability Expectations, Gap]

## Notes

- Use this checklist to test the quality of the written taxonomy requirements, not the correctness of any added WSDL fixture files.
- `[Gap]`, `[Ambiguity]`, `[Conflict]`, `[Assumption]`, and `[Dependency]` markers highlight places where the current documents may need stronger wording before implementation tasks are generated.
- Taxonomy clarification pass completed on 2026-04-06 after tightening category boundaries, precedence rules, naming allowances, and taxonomy stability language across the spec, contract, and research artifacts.
